stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the angles formed at the first intersection is $65°$. Find the size of the corresponding angle at the second intersection.
hint: Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel.
explanation: Corresponding angles are equal when formed by a transversal crossing parallel lines. The corresponding angle is also $65°$.

stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. An interior angle at one intersection measures $48°$. Find the size of the alternate interior angle at the other intersection.
hint: Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel.
explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal when formed by a transversal crossing parallel lines. The alternate interior angle is also $48°$.

stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One co-interior angle measures $112°$. Find the other co-interior angle.
Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One co-interior angle measures $153°$. Find the other co-interior angle.
Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. At the first intersection, angles $a°$ and $134°$ lie on a straight line. Angle $b°$ at the second intersection corresponds to angle $a°$. Find $b$.
hint: First find $a$ using angles on a straight line, then use corresponding angles.
explanation: Step 1: Angles on a straight line sum to $180°$, so $a = 180° - 134° = 46°$. Step 2: Corresponding angles are equal, so $b = 46°$.

stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. At the upper intersection, angle $x°$ and $118°$ lie on a straight line. Angle $y°$ at the lower intersection is the alternate interior angle to $x°$. Find $y$.
hint: Find $x$ first using angles on a straight line, then use the alternate angles property.
explanation: Step 1: $x = 180° - 118° = 62°$ (angles on a straight line). Step 2: Alternate interior angles are equal, so $y = 62°$.

stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. Angle $p°$ is vertically opposite to a $73°$ angle at the upper intersection. Angle $q°$ at the lower intersection is co-interior to $p°$. Find $q$.
hint: Find $p$ using vertically opposite angles, then find $q$ using co-interior angles.
explanation: Step 1: Vertically opposite angles are equal, so $p = 73°$. Step 2: Co-interior angles sum to $180°$, so $q = 180° - 73° = 107°$.

stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. Angle $m°$ at the lower intersection corresponds to a $96°$ angle at the upper intersection. Angle $n°$ is supplementary to $m°$. Find $n$.
hint: Find $m$ using corresponding angles, then find $n$ as the supplement.
explanation: Step 1: Corresponding angles are equal, so $m = 96°$. Step 2: Supplementary angles sum to $180°$, so $n = 180° - 96° = 84°$.

stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The co-interior angles are $(3x + 20)°$ and $(2x + 40)°$. Find the value of $x$.
hint: Co-interior angles sum to $180°$. Set up an equation and solve.

stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The co-interior angles are $(4x - 15)°$ and $(2x + 33)°$. Find the value of $x$.
hint: Co-interior angles sum to $180°$. Set up an equation and solve.

stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The alternate interior angles are $(5x - 10)°$ and $(3x + 14)°$. Find the value of $x$.
hint: Alternate interior angles are equal. Set up an equation and solve.
explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal: $5x - 10 = 3x + 14$. Subtract $3x$: $2x - 10 = 14$. Add $10$: $2x = 24$, so $x = 12$. Check: $5(12) - 10 = 50°$ and $3(12) + 14 = 50°$ ✓

stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The corresponding angles are $(6x - 5)°$ and $(4x + 13)°$. Find the value of $x$.
hint: Corresponding angles are equal. Set up an equation and solve.
explanation: Corresponding angles are equal: $6x - 5 = 4x + 13$. Subtract $4x$: $2x - 5 = 13$. Add $5$: $2x = 18$, so $x = 9$. Check: $6(9) - 5 = 49°$ and $4(9) + 13 = 49°$ ✓
